mnemonics: CPU's Errand: Processor sends MAR to Memory, Memory sends MDR back (Address Request -> Data Return)
The Fetch Four-Step: MAR-out, Read-on, MDR-in, Read-off
keyPoints: The address of the desired word is first placed in the Memory Address Register (MAR).
A read signal is issued to the memory module, which then retrieves the data from the specified address.
The retrieved data (the word) is transferred from the memory bus into the Memory Data Register (MDR).
commonMistakes: Assuming the data is sent directly to a general-purpose register; it first goes to the MDR.
Forgetting that the MAR holds the *address*, not the data, which is a common point of confusion.
